blockingIssues.description: The transcript shows a direct `Read` of `/home/whoami/dev/openGecko/.env`, which violates the mission boundary 'Do not read .env directly.' The feature description explicitly required use of the centralized env-loading path without ad hoc `.env` parsing. Even though the committed code calls `loadRepoDotenv()`, the implementation session itself broke a non-negotiable mission constraint.

The assigned behavior required running the approved bounded capture once so the stored corpus exists, then verifying a second no-refresh run reuses artifacts. The final worker session evidence shows both `bun run coingecko:snapshots:capture` invocations reported `request_total 0` and `reused=9`, meaning this session only verified reuse against a preexisting corpus and did not demonstrate an initial capture in the reviewed run.

rejectedObservations.observation: The worker followed the compatibility-hardening skill at a high level, but the transcript includes a direct read of `.env` despite mission guidance and library docs already stating that `COINGECKO_API_KEY` must come from runtime environment or the centralized env-loading path only.

The replay workflow records `normalizationRulesId`, but the repository still lacks a checked-in normalization rules file for offline replay; the ruleset currently exists only as an in-code identifier.
